memes.description: "That, in the resurrection, the substance of our bodies, however disintegrated, shall be entirely reunited."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 21: XXI"
memes.description: "Of the new spiritual body into which the flesh of the saints shall be transformed."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 22: XXII"
memes.description: "Of the miseries and ills to which the human race is justly exposed through the first sin, and from which none can be delivered save by Christ’s grace."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 23: XXIII"
memes.description: "Of the miseries of this life which attach peculiarly to the toil of good men, irrespective of those which are common to the good and bad."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 24: XXIV"
memes.description: "Of the blessings with which the Creator has filled this life, obnoxious though it be to the curse."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 25: XXV"
memes.description: "Of the obstinacy of those individuals who impugn the resurrection of the body, though, as was predicted, the whole world believes it."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 26: XXVI"
memes.description: "That the opinion of Porphyry, that the soul, in order to be blessed, must be separated from every kind of body, is demolished by Plato, who says that the sup..."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 27: XXVII"
memes.description: "Of the apparently conflicting opinions of Plato and Porphyry, which would have conducted them both to the truth if they could have yielded to one another."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 28: XXVIII"
memes.description: "What Plato or Labeo, or even Varro, might have contributed to the true faith of the resurrection, if they had adopted one another’s opinions into one scheme."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 29: XXIX"
memes.description: "Of the beatific vision."

memes.label: "Book 22 — Ch. 30: XXX"
memes.description: "Of the eternal felicity of the city of God, and of the perpetual Sabbath."
